name: Using the Docs
description: Learn to navigate the v0 documentation effectively.

steps.title: Open the Search
steps.task: Press Ctrl+K (or Cmd+K on Mac) to open the search dialog. You can also click the search button in the header.
steps.hint: Look for the search icon in the top navigation bar
steps.learn: How to quickly access the search dialog using keyboard shortcuts

steps.title: Search for something
steps.task: Type "Tabs" in the search box to find the Tabs component documentation and then press Enter.
steps.hint: The search uses fuzzy matching, so partial words work too
steps.learn: How to use fuzzy search to find components and documentation

steps.title: Open Ask AI
steps.task: Press Ctrl+/ (or Cmd+/ on Mac) to open Ask AI, or click the input at the bottom of the page.
steps.hint: Ask AI knows the content of the current page and can answer questions about it
steps.learn: How to access the AI assistant for context-aware help

steps.title: Close Ask AI
steps.task: Click the close button to dismiss the Ask AI panel.
steps.learn: How to dismiss the Ask AI panel when done

steps.title: Reopen Ask AI
steps.task: Click the Ask AI input at the bottom to bring back your conversation. Notice it remembers what you discussed!
steps.hint: Your conversation persists until you clear it or refresh the page
steps.learn: That conversations persist and can be resumed

steps.title: Explore save options
steps.task: Notice the toolbar buttons: save to Vuetify Bin for sharing, copy the conversation, or reset to start fresh. Click the close button to dismiss the Ask AI panel.
steps.hint: Vuetify Bin creates a shareable link to your conversation
steps.learn: How to save, share, and manage AI conversations

steps.title: Open Settings
steps.task: Click the settings button in the header to open the Settings panel.
steps.hint: Skill filters change the visible navigation items
steps.learn: Where to find site-wide settings and preferences

steps.title: Adjust your skill level
steps.task: Select one or more skill levels to filter documentation by complexity. Beginner shows fundamentals, Advanced reveals in-depth content.
steps.hint: Try toggling levels to see how navigation updates
steps.learn: How skill levels filter documentation to match your experience
